rulings.Case Title: DALLAS UDOVCH VS. WESTBURNE SUPPLY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 23, 2024, in Department 301, Line 1. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File Second Amended Complaint is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 473(a)(1). No opposition filed. Plaintiff shall file and serve the Second Amended Complaint forthwith on the newly added defendants PARAMOUNT GLOBAL (Previously sued as Doe 3) and K-M INDUSTRIES HOLDING CO. INC. (previously sued as Doe 65), as well as on those defendants who have not yet appeared in the case. The Second Amended Complaint is deemed served on all other Defendants who have previously appeared in the action. It is further ordered that Austyn Udovch, Spenser Udovch, and Dennison Udovch, by and through his Guardian ad Litem Koen Lav, be added as wrongful death heirs and plaintiffs to this action. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on July 23, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. =(301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: JUDITH POWERS VS. UNION OIL COMPANY OF CALLIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 23, 2024, in Department 301, Line 2. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 473(a)(1). No opposition filed. Plaintiff shall file and serve the First Amended Complaint forthwith on the newly added defendants: (i) Paramount Global (previously sued as Doe 1); (ii) General Electric Company (previously sued as Doe 2); (iii) Sun Ship, LLC (previously sued as Doe 3); (iv) Metropolitan Life Insurance Company (previously sued as Doe 4); (v) Hopeman Brothers, Inc. (previously sued as Does 5 & 301); (vi) Consolidated Insulation, Inc. (previously sued as Does 6 & 302); (vii) Anheuserbusch, LLC (previously sued as Doe 303); (viii) Texaco Inc. (previously sued as Doe 304); (ix) International Business Machines Corporation (previously sued as Doe 305), as well as on those defendants who have not yet appeared in the case. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served on all other Defendants who have previously appeared in the action. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on July 23, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. =(301/RCE)
